title: TypeScript
description: CReact is written in TypeScript. Here's how to get the most out of the type system for signals, props, and components.
tsconfig_intro: The key setting is <0>jsxImportSource</0>. This tells TypeScript to use CReact's JSX types:
value_or_accessor_intro: The site publisher's <0>SiteObjectProps</0> types <1>bucket</1> and <2>entry</2> as <0>MaybeAccessor</0>, so a single interface accepts both plain values and getters and the handler stays fully typed after access unwraps them.
typed_defaults_intro: The tenant fleet's <0>TenantTreeProps</0> marks <1>plan</1> optional, then <2>mergeProps</2> supplies the default and narrows the result to a fully-required shape the rest of the tree can rely on.
info_types: CReact re-exports key types: <0>Accessor</0>, <1>Setter</1>, <2>MaybeAccessor</2>, <3>SignalOptions</3>, <4>MemoOptions</4>, <5>Owner</5>, <6>Context</6>, and all runtime types.
in_the_wild: See these typed props provision real resources in the <0>site-publisher walkthrough</0>.
